Title: JA-Mediated Regulation of Amino Acid Homeostasis Adjusts Metabolic Flux and Enhances Spider Mite Tolerance via the SlJAZ8-SlWRKY57-SlAVT6s Module in Tomato

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AVT family vacuolar amino acid transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed in most tissues; relatively low in developing fruits
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, especially associated with type VI glandular trichomes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes amino acid efflux from vacuole to cytosol, decreases free amino acid pools, increases terpene accumulation, gibberellin levels, type VI trichome density, growth, and spider mite tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; subcellular localization; yeast heterologous transport/efflux assay; overexpression; RNAi knockdown; metabolite profiling (amino acids, IPP/FPP/GGPP, terpenes, GA); spider mite preference/fecundity/inoculation assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spider mite infestation; MeJA treatment; paclobutrazol treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SlAVT6A knockdown caused dwarf phenotype and smaller leaves; overexpression-associated amino acid depletion may reallocate primary metabolism
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Terpenes contribute to anti-herbivore defense; gibberellin promotes type VI glandular trichome development and growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression or activation of SlAVT6A is a promising strategy to redirect amino acid flux toward defense terpenes and GA, improving spider mite tolerance while supporting growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as the primary vacuolar amino acid exporter; stronger efflux activity than SlAVT6B; co-expression with SlAVT6B reduces its transport output through heterodimerization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAVT6A was identified by amino-acid mGWAS and localizes to the tonoplast. 2. In yeast, SlAVT6A showed strong amino acid efflux activity for multiple amino acids including phenylalanine and tryptophan. 3. SlAVT6A overexpression lowered free amino acid levels and increased IPP/FPP/GGPP, volatile terpenes, bioactive GA, VI-type trichomes, growth, and spider mite tolerance.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AVT family vacuolar amino acid transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed mainly in mature flowers and shoots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, especially associated with type VI glandular trichomes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Antagonizes SlAVT6A transport capacity; overexpression decreases trichomes, terpenes, and spider mite tolerance, while knockout phenocopies SlAVT6A overexpression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; subcellular localization; yeast heterologous transport/efflux assay; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; CRISPR knockout; overexpression; metabolite profiling; spider mite assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spider mite infestation; MeJA treatment; paclobutrazol treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced defense-associated metabolites and resistance; knockout promoted growth-related traits
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Terpenes contribute to anti-herbivore defense; gibberellin promotes type VI glandular trichome development and growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function editing of SlAVT6B may relieve inhibition of SlAVT6A and improve defense metabolite accumulation and spider mite tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows weaker amino acid efflux than SlAVT6A but forms a preferential heterodimer with SlAVT6A, shrinking the transport pocket and reducing efflux activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAVT6B is a tonoplast AVT transporter with weaker amino acid efflux activity than SlAVT6A. 2. SlAVT6B physically interacts with SlAVT6A and reduces SlAVT6A-mediated amino acid export. 3. SlAVT6B knockout increased terpenes, GA, VI-type trichomes, growth, and spider mite tolerance, opposite to SlAVT6B overexpression.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY family transcription factor; W-box-binding protein
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression of SlAVT6A and direct transcriptional activation of SlAVT6B
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses SlAVT6A promoter activity and activates SlAVT6B promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Amino acid homeostasis and reallocation of amino acid flux into terpene and gibberellin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and type VI glandular trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of spider mite tolerance by increasing amino acid retention and reducing trichome density and terpene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking JA signaling to amino acid transport and downstream defense metabolite production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter analysis; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC; overexpression and knockdown transgenics; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling; spider mite assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a regulatory complex with SlJAZ8 that attenuates transactivation/repression of SlAVT6 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression lowered terpene content and VI-type trichome density, increasing susceptibility to spider mites
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for effector constructs and transgenic overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced at transcript level by MeJA; protein undergoes MeJA-induced degradation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Terpenes provide herbivore defense; gibberellin supports glandular trichome development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockdown or suppression of SlWRKY57 could enhance terpene/GA-linked defense and spider mite tolerance; should be combined with SlAVT6A activation or SlAVT6B editing
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MeJA increased SlWRKY57 transcript but promoted SlWRKY57 protein degradation; SlWRKY57 overexpression reduced monoterpenes and sesquiterpenes to about 50% of WT
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlWRKY57 directly binds W-box motifs in both SlAVT6A and SlAVT6B promoters. 2. SlWRKY57 represses SlAVT6A but activates SlAVT6B, shifting amino acid homeostasis away from defense metabolite production. 3. SlWRKY57 overexpression reduces VI-type trichomes and terpene levels and increases spider mite susceptibility, whereas knockdown enhances tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlWRKY57-mediated regulation of SlAVT6A and SlAVT6B
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated attenuation of SlWRKY57 transcriptional activity; associated with JA-responsive regulatory control
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Weakens SlWRKY57 responsiveness on SlAVT6A and SlAVT6B promoters
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA-linked control of amino acid homeostasis and downstream terpene/gibberellin-associated defense metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and type VI glandular trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Connects JA signaling to the SlWRKY57-SlAVT6 module, thereby modulating amino acid flux and spider mite tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-pathway gatekeeper that modulates the decisive TF SlWRKY57 and thereby alters defense metabolite output
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC/LCI; dual-LUC with SlWRKY57 and SlAVT6 promoters; MeJA-response assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; spider mite tolerance context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Terpenes defend against spider mites; gibberellin contributes to trichome development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering JA-signaling nodes such as SlJAZ8 may fine-tune the SlWRKY57-SlAVT6 module for inducible defense metabolite enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes SlJAZ8 as part of the regulatory module, but no standalone transgenic phenotyping of SlJAZ8 was reported in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlJAZ8 physically interacts with SlWRKY57. 2. SlJAZ8 attenuates SlWRKY57-dependent regulation of SlAVT6A and SlAVT6B promoters in dual-LUC assays. 3. SlJAZ8 links JA signaling to amino acid homeostasis and downstream defense metabolism in the proposed module.
